result:
**Goal: Add Langton's Ant and generalized turmites as a new simulation mode.**

This is the one iconic cellular automaton variant conspicuously missing from the project. Unlike every existing mode (which updates all cells simultaneously via field rules), Langton's Ant is *agent-based* — one or more "ants" walk the grid, flipping cell colors and turning based on the cell they stand on. It produces the famous emergent "highway" after ~10,000 chaotic steps, and generalized turmites (multi-state, multi-color) produce stunning symmetric patterns. It would add a fundamentally different computational paradigm to the simulator while fitting cleanly into the existing architecture (mode switching, topology support, braille rendering, time travel, etc.).

Want me to implement it?
